name: efficientformer

members.name: EfficientFormerConfig
members.signature: EfficientFormerConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, depths: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 2, 6, 4), embed_dims: tuple[int, ...] = (48, 96, 224, 448), mlp_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0), num_vit: int = 1, pool_size: int = 3, key_dim: int = 32, num_heads: int = 8, attn_ratio: float = 4.0, resolution: int = 7, drop_path_rate: float = 0.0, layer_scale_init: float = 1e-05)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, depths: tuple[int, ...] = (3, 2, 6, 4), embed_dims: tuple[int, ...] = (48, 96, 224, 448), mlp_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0), num_vit: int = 1, pool_size: int = 3, key_dim: int = 32, num_heads: int = 8, attn_ratio: float = 4.0, resolution: int = 7, drop_path_rate: float = 0.0, layer_scale_init: float = 1e-05)

members.summary: Configuration dataclass for every EfficientFormer variant.
members.extended:
`EfficientFormerConfig` is an immutable container that fully
specifies the architecture of an EfficientFormer (Li et al., 2022).
The model is a *mobile-grade* vision backbone whose on-device
latency, rather than its FLOP count, matches MobileNet on the same
hardware while retaining transformer-level accuracy.

Stages 1-3 use *pooling mixer* MetaFormer blocks (a parameter-free
$3 \times 3$ average pool replacing self-attention) and
operate entirely in $(B, C, H, W)$ layout.  Stage 4 is the
only stage that pays the cost of a reshape and runs standard
multi-head self-attention on the now-tiny token grid.

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes for the classification head.  Defaults
to `1000` (ImageNet-1k).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of input image channels.  Defaults to `3` (RGB).

parameters.name: depths
parameters.description:
Number of blocks per stage.  Defaults to `(3, 2, 6, 4)`
(EfficientFormer-L1).

parameters.name: embed_dims
parameters.description:
Output channel width per stage.  Defaults to
`(48, 96, 224, 448)` (EfficientFormer-L1).

parameters.name: mlp_ratios
parameters.description:
Per-stage MLP expansion ratio.  Defaults to
`(4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0)`.

parameters.name: num_vit
parameters.description:
Number of trailing blocks in the *last* stage that run 3-D
multi-head self-attention (the remainder run the 4-D pooling
token mixer).  Defaults to `1` (EfficientFormer-L1); L3 uses
`4` and L7 uses `8`.

parameters.name: pool_size
parameters.description:
Spatial extent of the average-pool token mixer in the 4-D
pooling blocks.  Defaults to `3`.

parameters.name: key_dim
parameters.description:
Per-head query/key dimension in the attention token mixer.
Defaults to `32` (fixed across all paper variants).

parameters.name: num_heads
parameters.description:
Number of attention heads in the attention token mixer.
Defaults to `8` (fixed across all paper variants).

parameters.name: attn_ratio
parameters.description:
Value/key dimension ratio in the attention token mixer; the
per-head value dimension is `attn_ratio * key_dim`.  Defaults
to `4.0`.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description:
Side length of the (square) token grid feeding the attention
stage; the learned attention-bias table has
`resolution ** 2` entries per head.  Defaults to `7`
(a `7 x 7` grid at the default `224` input).

parameters.name: drop_path_rate
parameters.description:
Maximum stochastic-depth rate; linearly scheduled across all
blocks of the trunk.  The paper uses `0.0` for L1, `0.1`
for L3, `0.2` for L7.  Defaults to `0.0`.

parameters.name: layer_scale_init
parameters.description:
Initial value of the layer-scale parameter on every residual
branch.  Defaults to `1e-5` (Li et al., 2022, appendix).

examples:
Build an EfficientFormer-L1 configuration with a 100-class head:
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import EfficientFormerConfig
>>> cfg = EfficientFormerConfig(num_classes=100)
>>> cfg.depths, cfg.embed_dims, cfg.num_classes
((3, 2, 6, 4), (48, 96, 224, 448), 100)
notes:
The canonical variants registered as factory functions in
`lucid.models.vision.efficientformer` are:

============================ ================ ===================
Variant                      depths           embed_dims
============================ ================ ===================
EfficientFormer-L1           (3, 2, 6, 4)     (48, 96, 224, 448)
EfficientFormer-L3           (4, 4, 12, 6)    (64, 128, 320, 512)
EfficientFormer-L7           (6, 6, 18, 8)    (96, 192, 384, 768)
============================ ================ ===================

Reference: Yanyu Li *et al.*, *"EfficientFormer: Vision
Transformers at MobileNet Speed"*, NeurIPS 2022,
[arXiv:2206.01191](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.01191).

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: Constant string `"efficientformer"` used by the model registry.

members.name: EfficientFormer
members.extended:
EfficientFormer is a *mobile-grade* vision backbone designed so
that its on-device latency, rather than its FLOP count, matches
MobileNet on the same hardware while retaining transformer-level
accuracy.  The trunk has four stages preceded by a two stride-2
convolutional stem.  Stages 1-3 are MetaFormer-style *pooling*
blocks operating in $(B, C, H, W)$ layout — no reshape, no
self-attention:

$$
\begin{aligned}
x &\leftarrow x + \gamma_1 \odot \bigl(
    \mathrm{AvgPool}_{3 \times 3}(x) - x\bigr), \\
x &\leftarrow x + \gamma_2 \odot \mathrm{ConvMLP}(x),
\end{aligned}
$$

with $\gamma_1, \gamma_2$ initialised to $10^{-5}$
(CaiT-style layer scale).  The last stage switches its trailing
$\texttt{num\_vit}$ blocks to standard multi-head
self-attention with a learned relative-position bias on the
now-tiny token grid.

`forward_features` returns the mean-pooled
$(B, \texttt{embed\_dims[-1]})$ feature.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen dataclass specifying `depths`, `embed_dims`,
`mlp_ratios`, `num_vit`, `drop_path_rate`,
`layer_scale_init`, `in_channels`, and `num_classes`.
See `EfficientFormerConfig`.

examples:
Build an EfficientFormer-L1 backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import (
...     EfficientFormer, EfficientFormerConfig,
... )
>>> model = EfficientFormer(EfficientFormerConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape                       # (B, embed_dims[-1])
(1, 448)
notes:
Reference: Yanyu Li *et al.*, *"EfficientFormer: Vision
Transformers at MobileNet Speed"*, NeurIPS 2022,
[arXiv:2206.01191](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.01191).

attributes.name: stem
attributes.description:
Two stride-2 $3 \times 3$ convolutions reducing the
input by 4x.

attributes.name: stages
attributes.description:
Four stages — three pooling stages and one mixed
pooling/attention stage; stages 1-3 carry a downsample.

attributes.name: norm
attributes.description: Final LayerNorm applied to the channel-last token sequence.

members.name: EfficientFormerForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: EfficientFormer with a distilled dual classification head (Li et al., 2022).
members.extended:
Wraps the same trunk as `EfficientFormer` and adds a final
LayerNorm, a mean pool over tokens, and *two* linear heads — the
classification head and the distillation head — whose logits are
averaged at inference:

$$
\text{logits} = \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl(
    W_{\text{head}}\,z + b_{\text{head}}
    + W_{\text{dist}}\,z + b_{\text{dist}}\bigr),
\qquad
z = \mathrm{Mean}(\mathrm{LN}(z^{L})).
$$

The published EfficientFormer checkpoints were trained with hard
knowledge distillation (DeiT-style), so reproducing the reported
top-1 accuracy requires averaging both heads.

Pass `labels` to `forward` to compute the cross-entropy
loss in the same pass.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture specification.  Must set `num_classes` to the
desired number of output categories.  See
`EfficientFormerConfig`.

examples:
End-to-end inference with the default EfficientFormer-L1
classifier:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import (
...     EfficientFormerConfig, EfficientFormerForImageClassification,
... )
>>> model = EfficientFormerForImageClassification(EfficientFormerConfig())
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Li *et al.*, *"EfficientFormer: Vision Transformers at
MobileNet Speed"*, NeurIPS 2022.  EfficientFormer-L1 / L3 / L7
reach **79.2 / 82.4 / 83.3 % top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at MobileNetV2-
class on-device latency (Li et al., 2022, Table 4).

attributes.name: stages
attributes.description: Three pooling stages + one mixed pooling/attention stage.

attributes.name: head_dist
attributes.description:
Distillation head `(num_classes, embed_dims[-1])`; averaged
with `head` at inference.

members.name: efficientformer_l1
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *EfficientFormer-L1* configuration:
`depths=(3, 2, 6, 4)`, `embed_dims=(48, 96, 224, 448)`,
`mlp_ratios=(4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0)`, `drop_path_rate=0.0`.
Approximately **12.3M parameters** — the smallest, lowest-latency
variant in the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides on top of the canonical L1 config.  Each
override must match a field of `EfficientFormerConfig`.

returns.description:
An `EfficientFormer` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 448)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import efficientformer_l1
>>> model = efficientformer_l1()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 448)
notes:
EfficientFormer-L1 reaches **79.2% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at
MobileNetV2-class on-device latency (Li et al., 2022, Table 4).
See [arXiv:2206.01191](https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.01191).

members.name: efficientformer_l1_cls
members.signature: efficientformer_l1_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: EfficientFormerL1Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `efficientformer_l1` backbone with a final
LayerNorm, a mean pool over tokens, and a distilled dual head
(`head` + `head_dist`) averaged at inference.  Default output is
`num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).  ~12.3M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag
(`EfficientFormerL1Weights.SNAP_DIST_IN1K`); a tag string
→ that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with `weights`
(which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical L1 config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import efficientformer_l1_cls
>>> model = efficientformer_l1_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
EfficientFormer-L1 reaches **79.2% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** at
MobileNetV2-class latency (Li et al., 2022, Table 4).  Pretrained
weights are converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l1.snap_dist_in1k` and hosted under
`lucid-dl/efficientformer-l1`.

members.name: efficientformer_l3
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *EfficientFormer-L3* configuration:
`depths=(4, 4, 12, 6)`, `embed_dims=(64, 128, 320, 512)`,
`drop_path_rate=0.1`.  Approximately **30.9M parameters**.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical L3 config.

returns.description:
An `EfficientFormer` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 512)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import efficientformer_l3
>>> model = efficientformer_l3()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 512)
notes:
EfficientFormer-L3 reaches **82.4% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Li
et al., 2022, Table 4).

members.name: efficientformer_l3_cls
members.signature: efficientformer_l3_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: EfficientFormerL3Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `efficientformer_l3` backbone (`depths=
(4, 4, 12, 6)`, `embed_dims=(64, 128, 320, 512)`) with a final
LayerNorm, mean pool, and a distilled dual head (`head` +
`head_dist`) averaged at inference.  ~31.4M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag
(`EfficientFormerL3Weights.SNAP_DIST_IN1K`); a tag string
→ that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with `weights`
(which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical L3 config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import efficientformer_l3_cls
>>> model = efficientformer_l3_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
EfficientFormer-L3 reaches **82.4% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Li
et al., 2022, Table 4).  Pretrained weights are converted from
timm's `efficientformer_l3.snap_dist_in1k` and hosted under
`lucid-dl/efficientformer-l3`.

members.name: efficientformer_l7
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *EfficientFormer-L7* configuration:
`depths=(6, 6, 18, 8)`, `embed_dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`,
`drop_path_rate=0.2`.  Approximately **81.5M parameters** — the
largest variant in the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical L7 config.

returns.description:
An `EfficientFormer` backbone returning a flat
$(B, 768)$ feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import efficientformer_l7
>>> model = efficientformer_l7()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 768)
notes:
EfficientFormer-L7 reaches **83.3% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Li
et al., 2022, Table 4) — the headline result of the paper.

members.name: efficientformer_l7_cls
members.signature: efficientformer_l7_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: EfficientFormerL7Weights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.extended:
Combines the `efficientformer_l7` backbone (`depths=
(6, 6, 18, 8)`, `embed_dims=(96, 192, 384, 768)`) with a final
LayerNorm, mean pool, and a distilled dual head (`head` +
`head_dist`) averaged at inference.  ~82.2M parameters — the
largest EfficientFormer variant.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag
(`EfficientFormerL7Weights.SNAP_DIST_IN1K`); a tag string
→ that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with `weights`
(which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical L7 config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.efficientformer import efficientformer_l7_cls

>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
EfficientFormer-L7 reaches **83.3% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Li
et al., 2022, Table 4) — the headline result of the paper.
Pretrained weights are converted from timm's

members.name: EfficientFormerL1Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l1.snap_dist_in1k` (Li et al., 2022; ~12.3M params,
79.2% top-1).

members.name: EfficientFormerL3Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l3.snap_dist_in1k` (Li et al., 2022; ~31.4M params,
82.4% top-1).

members.name: EfficientFormerL7Weights
members.extended:
Single ImageNet-1k checkpoint converted from timm's
`efficientformer_l7.snap_dist_in1k` (Li et al., 2022; ~82.2M params,
83.3% top-1).

citation: Li, Yanyu, et al. "EfficientFormer: Vision Transformers at MobileNet Speed." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, vol. 35, 2022.
theory:
EfficientFormer is a *mobile-grade* vision backbone designed so
that its end-to-end on-device latency, rather than its FLOP count,
matches the latency of MobileNet on the same hardware while
retaining transformer-level accuracy.  The authors profile real
iOS / NPU latency and identify three transformer operators that
are unexpectedly slow: the reshape between spatial and token
layouts, GeLU compared to ReLU, and LayerNorm compared to
BatchNorm.  EfficientFormer is the result of a *latency-aware*
architecture search that designs around these costs.

The backbone is a four-stage pyramid.  Stages 1-3 use *pooling
mixer* blocks — a MetaFormer-style token mixer that replaces the
quadratic self-attention with a parameter-free $3 \times 3$
average pooling — and operate entirely in the 4-D
$(B, C, H, W)$ layout so no expensive reshape is needed.
Each pooling block computes



$$
x \leftarrow x + \gamma_1 \odot \bigl(\mathrm{AvgPool}_{3 \times 3}(x) - x\bigr),
\qquad
x \leftarrow x + \gamma_2 \odot \mathrm{MLP}(x),
$$

with $\gamma_1, \gamma_2$ initialised to $10^{-5}$ —
the *layer scale* trick from CaiT.  Stage 4 is the only stage that
pays the cost of a reshape and runs standard multi-head
self-attention with relative position bias on the now-tiny token
grid.  The resulting design (variants L1 / L3 / L7) matches
ResNet-50 / DeiT-S accuracy at MobileNetV2-class latency on an
iPhone 12 NPU.
